name: Monk
description: Monks are masters of martial arts, harnessing the power of body, mind, and spirit. Through discipline and training, they channel their Focus to perform extraordinary feats of combat and athleticism.
weapon_proficiencies: Martial weapons that have the Light property
tool_proficiencies: Choose one type of artisan's tools or one musical instrument

1.Martial Arts: You gain the following benefits while you are unarmed or wielding Monk weapons (Simple Melee weapons and Martial weapons with the Light property). Bonus Unarmed Strike: You can make one Unarmed Strike as a Bonus Action. Dexterous Attacks: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of Strength for attack and damage rolls of your Unarmed Strikes and Monk weapons. Martial Arts Die: Your Unarmed Strike uses a d6 for damage. This die changes as you gain Monk levels: d8 at level 5, d10 at level 11, d12 at level 17.

Unarmored Defense.description: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, your base AC equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Wisdom modifiers.

2.Monk's Focus: You gain a number of Focus Points equal to your Monk level, which you can spend on Flurry of Blows (1 Focus Point: make two Unarmed Strikes as a Bonus Action after taking the Attack action), Patient Defense (1 Focus Point: take the Dodge action as a Bonus Action), and Step of the Wind (1 Focus Point: take the Disengage or Dash action as a Bonus Action, and your jump distance is doubled for the turn). You regain all Focus Points when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

Unarmored Movement.description: Your Speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield. This bonus increases as you gain Monk levels: 15 ft at level 6, 20 ft at level 10, 25 ft at level 14, 30 ft at level 18.

2.Uncanny Metabolism: When you roll Initiative, you can regain all expended Focus Points. When you do so, you also regain a number of Hit Points equal to your Martial Arts die plus your Monk level. You can use this feature only once per Long Rest.

3.Deflect Attacks: When an attack roll hits you and its damage includes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can take a Reaction to reduce the total damage by 1d10 plus your Dexterity modifier plus your Monk level. If the damage is reduced to 0, you can spend 1 Focus Point to redirect the attack back. Make a ranged attack (range 20/60) against a target within range using the same attack roll modifier. On hit, the target takes damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die plus your Dexterity modifier. The damage type matches the original attack.
3.Monk Subclass: Choose a Monk subclass: Warrior of Mercy, Warrior of Shadow, Warrior of the Elements, or Warrior of the Open Hand.

4.Ability Score Improvement: Increase one ability score by 2, or increase two ability scores by 1, to a maximum of 20. Alternatively, choose a feat.
4.Slow Fall: You can take a Reaction when you fall to reduce any falling damage you take by an amount equal to five times your Monk level.

5.Extra Attack: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
5.Stunning Strike: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a Monk weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can spend 1 Focus Point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Wisdom modifier) or have the Stunned condition until the start of your next turn.

6.Empowered Strikes: Whenever you deal damage with your Unarmed Strike, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.

Unarmored Movement.description: Unarmored Movement speed bonus increases to 15 feet.

7.Evasion: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

8.Ability Score Improvement: Increase one ability score by 2, or increase two ability scores by 1, to a maximum of 20. Alternatively, choose a feat.

9.Acrobatic Movement: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the move.

10.Heightened Focus: Your Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind gain the following improvements. Flurry of Blows: You can make three Unarmed Strikes instead of two when you spend a Focus Point. Patient Defense: When you spend a Focus Point, you also gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die. Step of the Wind: When you spend a Focus Point, you can choose a willing creature within 5 feet of you that is your size or smaller. You move the creature with you until the end of your turn, and the creature's movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks.
10.Self-Restoration: You can spend 1 Focus Point to end one of the following conditions on yourself: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned.

Unarmored Movement.description: Unarmored Movement speed bonus increases to 20 feet.

12.Ability Score Improvement: Increase one ability score by 2, or increase two ability scores by 1, to a maximum of 20. Alternatively, choose a feat.

13.Deflect Energy: You can now use Deflect Attacks when the damage includes any damage type, not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing.

Disciplined Survivor.description: Your physical and mental discipline grant you proficiency in all saving throws. Additionally, when you fail a saving throw, you can spend 1 Focus Point to reroll it, and you must use the new result.

Unarmored Movement.description: Unarmored Movement speed bonus increases to 25 feet.

15.Perfect Focus: When you have 0 Focus Points after rolling Initiative, you regain 4 Focus Points. Also, your Heightened Focus improvements increase: Flurry of Blows makes three Unarmed Strikes without expending a Focus Point.

16.Ability Score Improvement: Increase one ability score by 2, or increase two ability scores by 1, to a maximum of 20. Alternatively, choose a feat.

Superior Defense.description: Your mastery of Focus allows you to bolster your body. You have Resistance to all damage types except Force.

Unarmored Movement.description: Unarmored Movement speed bonus increases to 30 feet.

19.Epic Boon: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

Body and Mind.description: You have honed your body and mind to new heights. Your Dexterity and Wisdom scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.
